batch_summary: Rachel excitedly shares the romantic details of her kiss with Ross to Monica and Phoebe, who celebrate enthusiastically. In contrast, Ross gives Joey and Chandler a brief, blunt summary of the same event. Later at the coffee shop, Chandler shows off his new laptop while Monica searches for a job. When Ross arrives, he admits that despite the great kiss, he is plagued by guilt after seeing his girlfriend Julie's saline solution at home, realizing the complicated situation he has created.
cumulative_summary: Rachel returns to the apartment to excitedly announce to Monica and Phoebe that Ross kissed her. The three women celebrate with ecstatic jumping and wine, as Rachel vividly describes the romantic and intense details of the encounter. Meanwhile, Ross recounts the same kiss to Chandler and Joey in a hilariously brief and straightforward manner over pizza. Later at the coffee shop, Chandler proudly shows off his expensive new laptop, which he admits he will only use for games, while Monica struggles to find a new cooking job. The mood shifts when Ross arrives, visibly stressed. Although he initially thought the night was great, he explains that returning home to find his girlfriend Julie's saline solution on his nightstand triggered a wave of intense guilt, leaving him deeply conflicted about his feelings and the complicated romantic dilemma he now faces.

batch_summary: Ross expresses his deep conflict to his friends, torn between his stable relationship with Julie and his decade-long dream of being with Rachel. The tension peaks when both Rachel and Julie arrive at the coffee shop, leading to an incredibly awkward encounter. Ross tries to overcompensate by acting overly affectionate with Julie, while Joey nearly exposes the saline solution dilemma. The atmosphere becomes hilariously strained when Phoebe takes the stage to perform a pointed new song about a love triangle titled "Two of Them Kissed Last Night."
